findings.claim: ggpaintr has TWO parallel data-source widget/resolution implementations — the single-instance/formula-local path (`ptr_setup_source_uis` in paintr-server.R) and the multi-instance shared-coordinator HOST path (`ptr_setup_panel_sources` in paintr-shared-ui.R) — and a fix applied to one does not propagate to the other.
findings.detail: This is the root structural reason ADR-0025-area fixes (#7 fileInput reset, source_file_reset flag, file_reset gate) landed only single-instance and had to be separately ported. The host path mirrors the single path's renderUI/registry-dispatch shape but is a distinct code path with its own renderUI, its own resolve_upload_source call, and its own mutex.
findings.verification: Both functions exist: ptr_setup_source_uis defined at server.R:2278, ptr_setup_panel_sources at shared-ui.R:482. Distinct files, each with its own renderUI + resolve_upload_source call + mutex. Line drift minor (file grew to 3630).

findings.claim: At coordinator HOST scope `ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis` is called with `state=NULL` (by ADR-0006 design), so its entire `if (!is.null(state))` block — including the `req(state$bound_names[[id]]())` source-ready/suppress gate — never executes; the host path instead drives the picker off `panel_sources[[sid]]()` reactives + a synthetic host_env.
findings.detail: Consequence: the single-instance suppress-on-vacate gate is structurally absent at host scope. The same binder runs at per-instance embedded scope WITH a real state, so behaviour diverges by call site, not by code. Reached via ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers (server.R:3269) from both the single-instance/embedded server (state passed) and the coordinator host (state omitted).
findings.source: R/paintr-server.R:2891-3256 (ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis), :3095-3156 (if(!is.null(state)) block); R/paintr-shared-ui.R:386 (host call omits state=)
findings.verification: ptr_bind_local_shared_consumers (server.R:3269) has `state = NULL` default; host call at shared-ui.R:386 omits `state=` → NULL propagates into ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis (server.R:2891). The `if (!is.null(state))` block begins at server.R:3095 and contains `shiny::req(state$bound_names[[...]]())` — wholly skipped when state is NULL. Confirmed by source read.

findings.claim: The downstream consumer's new-source-clear is edge-triggered on an identity string `sid#datapath#boundName`; an upload settles in TWO reactive render beats, and the clear only STICKS if the identity keeps changing across both beats — single-instance's `boundName` (from `state$bound_names`) flips `typed_ds→df_<hash>` so both beats re-clear, whereas at host scope `state=NULL` forces `boundName=""` permanently, the identity stabilises after beat 1, and beat 2 re-seeds the stale `current` via `boot_seed_selected` and REVERTS the clear.
findings.detail: Static mechanism confirmed in source: consumer_upstream_source_state builds identity = paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn) joined by '||', edge-triggered in consumer_clear_for_new_source via `src_seen && !identical(identity,last_identity) && user_supplied`. The sid→layer_name bound_names fallback (server.R:2576-2579) means a NULL state yields bn="" permanently. The TWO-beat runtime divergence (single clears, host reverts) was AppDriver-observed in-session (.scratch/ppupload7-probes/) and NOT re-run this pass; the static code path is fully consistent with it. Refutes an earlier 'req-gate serialization' theory.
findings.verification: consumer_upstream_source_state at server.R:2541 builds `paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn)`; consumer_clear_for_new_source at :2617 is edge-triggered (changed_since_last + first_render_on_upload); boot_seed_selected at :2478; bound_names sid→layer_name fallback at :2576-2579 confirms bn empties when state/bound_names absent. Runtime two-beat differential is probe-observed (not re-run), static path consistent.

findings.claim: Porting #7's source_file_reset flag to the host path is simpler than the single-instance original because in `ptr_setup_panel_sources` the renderUI, the shortcut, the flag, and the `resolve_upload_source` call all live in ONE `local({...})` scope — so the flag is a local `reactiveVal` read directly at the resolve call, with no cross-function plumbing, unlike single-instance which must publish on `state$source_file_reset` and read it back in the per-instance pipeline binders.
findings.detail: The planned host edits are additive in that one scope; resolve_upload_source already accepts `file_reset` (server.R:1251, `if (isTRUE(file_reset)) file_info <- NULL` at :1287). The host path uses a synthetic host_state list (eval_env + throwaway bound_names env + resolve_errors) at shared-ui.R:593-597 and returns reactive(slot()). Single-instance plumbing confirmed: file_reset_rv published on state$source_file_reset (server.R:2329-2330) and read back at :1536/:1614. Plan: dev/plans/0025-item7-followup-host-shared-consumer-clear/plan.html (exists).
findings.verification: resolve_upload_source signature carries `file_reset = FALSE` (server.R:1251) with `if (isTRUE(file_reset)) file_info <- NULL` (:1287). Single-instance publishes file_reset_rv on state$source_file_reset (:2329-2330) and reads at :1536/:1614. Host host_state list (eval_env/bound_names/resolve_errors) at shared-ui.R:593-597 with observe+resolve_upload_source in one loop-body scope at :602. 'Simpler' is a structurally-grounded judgment; plan dir exists.

findings.claim: A single-instance `ppVar()` consumer picker does NOT bind at boot — it stays absent from `input` until BOTH its upstream source has data (the `req(state$bound_names[[id]]())` gate clears) AND the layer's Controls subtab is opened; so a probe/test must provide the source first, then set `<layer>_subtab="Controls"`, then read the picker.
findings.detail: Single-instance source id pattern observed: `ggplot_0_ppUpload_NA`, shortcut `ggplot_0_ppUpload_NA_shortcut`; consumer picker id `ggplot_1_1_ppVar_NA` appeared only after typing an env source AND opening the subtab. Static mechanism (ptr_setup_consumer_uis suspend + req gate) confirmed; the specific runtime ids are probe-observed (discover.R is a thin 553-byte discovery script, not re-run this pass). Corroborated by project rule .claude/rules/testing.md (consumer var pickers are suspended under a renderUI subtab).
findings.verification: ptr_setup_consumer_uis defined at server.R:2625; consumer binder uses the req(state$bound_names[[...]]) suspend gate (shared block at :3095 / non-shared path). discover.R exists (.scratch/ppupload7-probes/single/discover.R). Runtime picker-id observation not re-run; mechanism + testing.md rule back it.

findings.claim: A consumer-clear regression test MUST select a column present in BOTH the pre- and post-switch datasets (e.g. `body_mass_g`), because a `selectInput`/`selectpicker` silently drops a selection that is no longer a valid option — so a test using a column unique to the old dataset passes even when the clear logic is broken (false green).
findings.detail: This trap masked the bug during static analysis: it looks 'cleared' merely because the option vanished, not because our clear fired. The probe deliberately used body_mass_g (present in both typed_ds and penguins.csv) to expose the persistence.
findings.verification: .scratch/ppupload7-probes/README.md:16 states verbatim: 'the consumer assertion MUST use a column present in BOTH datasets (body_mass_g) — a unique column is auto-dropped by selectInput and yields a false green.' Matches claim exactly.

findings.claim: To observe internal reactive state inside a shinytest2 child process, wrap the target ggpaintr internal in the fixture `app.R` via `assignInNamespace("fn", wrapper, ns="ggpaintr")` after `pkgload::load_all`, having the wrapper `message()` its state; `AppDriver$get_logs()` then captures it as stderr lines — parent-process `trace()` is invisible to the child.
findings.detail: Used this session to log consumer_upstream_source_state identity + consumer_clear_for_new_source verdict, producing the differential single-vs-host evidence. Capture the original with `ggpaintr:::fn` BEFORE reassigning. Parse via as.data.frame(app$get_logs())$message grep 'PROBE_'.
findings.verification: Both .scratch/ppupload7-probes/shared/app.R and single/app.R contain `assignInNamespace` + message() instrumentation. .claude/rules/serena-tools.md documents the same child-process assignInNamespace pattern (2 mentions). Matches claim.

findings.claim: The fileInput filename "pill" is rendered by the browser's native file widget and is NOT observable via chromote/CDP (`get_html`/`get_value`), so any success criterion about the pill clearing must be verified by a human EYEBALL in a real browser, never by an AppDriver assertion.
findings.detail: This is why #7 and its host-path follow-up carry a manual 'Gate 0' eyeball; the flag/vacate/consumer-clear pieces ARE harness-observable and carry the automated regression. A green shinytest2 run does not prove the pill cleared.
findings.verification: dev/plans/0025-item7-fileinput-reset/plan.html exists and references pill/chromote/eyeball/harness-blind/CDP (8 hits). Claim is a durable browser-behavior fact (native file widget chrome is outside the DOM chromote serializes); consistent with the plan's manual Gate-0 eyeball. Not independently re-run in a live browser this pass.

findings.claim: `/exec-plan <plan-dir>` only consumes a `/decision-to-plan` output directory — it reads `<plan-dir>/index.md` (merge order + parallel groups + worked-example matrix) and per-plan `NN-<slug>.html` files with HTML-comment frontmatter (`number`/`slug`/`depends_on`/`parallel_group`) + a `Files this plan owns:` block + a mandatory worked-example table, and REFUSES TO START if index.md or the worked-example section is missing.
findings.detail: A hand-authored single plan.html blessed only by /implementable is NOT exec-plan-ready (lacks index.md, frontmatter, NN-slug filename, worked-example table). exec-plan also runs autonomously, so a harness-blind eyeball success criterion cannot be grounded by it.
findings.verification: SKILL.md Inputs (lines ~31-34) require a /decision-to-plan dir with index.md + NN-<slug>.html. Steps ~99-105 confirm: reads index.md for merge order/parallel groups/worked-example matrix, extracts frontmatter number/slug/depends_on/parallel_group + 'Files this plan owns:' + worked-example table, and refuses to start if a plan is PASS by neither signal or the worked-example section is missing. Matches claim.

findings.claim: The working directory `/Users/willju/Research/ggpaintr-ppUpload-bug` is a git WORKTREE of the main repo at `/Users/willju/Research/ggpaintr`; git config like `core.hooksPath` resolves against the main repo's `.git`, which is why hook installs/checks must account for the main-repo path even when working in the worktree.
findings.detail: Relevant to any hook/config operation and to memory feedback-serena-project-root-doesnt-follow-worktree.
findings.source: git config core.hooksPath value; git worktree layout
findings.verification: `git worktree list` shows ggpaintr-ppUpload-bug as a worktree [ppUpload-bug] of main repo /Users/willju/Research/ggpaintr [main]; `git config core.hooksPath` = /Users/willju/Research/ggpaintr/.git/hooks (main repo's .git). Confirmed.

findings.claim: This repo's implementable enforcement hook is already installed via a chaining shim: `core.hooksPath` points at the MAIN repo's `.git/hooks`, whose `pre-commit` shim `exec`s `$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/dev/githooks/pre-commit` (the tracked enforcement hook), so it fires from the main repo AND every worktree without per-clone reinstall.
findings.detail: The hook hashes the staged plan blob via `printf '%s\n' "$(git show :f)" | grep -v 'implementable: PASS' | sha256 | cut -c1-12`; a current PASS stamp is required to commit any dev/plans/*.html|*.md or *-plan.md. For HTML the same pipeline over the unstamped worktree file reproduces the hook's hash exactly. Confirmed by committing the new plan (commit 38c48a1) cleanly.
findings.verification: /Users/willju/Research/ggpaintr/.git/hooks/pre-commit IS a chaining shim that resolves $root/dev/githooks/pre-commit (via git rev-parse --show-toplevel) and `exec`s it. dev/githooks/pre-commit exists and is executable. core.hooksPath confirmed pointing at the main repo .git/hooks. Commit 38c48a1 is HEAD on ppUpload-bug (clean commit corroborated). Hash-pipeline detail resides in the tracked hook (not re-derived this pass).
